source: Residential exposure (total insured value) by ZIP

source: Policies in force (PIF) by ZIP
notes: Policy counts; denominator side of penetration and the DIC gap.

source: Policies & exposure by category (wildfire risk score + distressed flag)

source: Written premium by category
notes: Average-premium and premium-burden figures; residential written premium anchored to ~$1.79B (±1%).

source: New / renewed / non-renewed policies + FAIR counts by ZIP
notes: Admitted-market denominator; total non-renewal rates; year-aligned penetration.

source: Fact sheets: insurer- vs insured-initiated non-renewal split
notes: The retreat signal. Split is not reported after 2021 (hence the series ends there).

source: P&C market share — homeowners line
notes: Homeowners direct written premium; the base for the $1B assessment allocation (statutory base year 2023).

source: $1B assessment — Commissioner's Order 2025-1
notes: The assessment itself: $1B total; allocated by market share; ≤50% recoupable from policyholders.

source: SB 824 moratorium bulletins (post-fire non-renewal freezes)
notes: Flags ZIP-years under a legal non-renewal freeze — explains dips in the retreat series.

source: Median household income (ACS B19013)
notes: Income variable driving every decile; household-weighted from tract to ZIP.

source: Median home value (ACS B25077)
notes: Owner-weighted to ZIP; premium proxy and value-decile input.

source: Households & tenure (ACS B25003)
notes: Occupied / owner-occupied units; the aggregation weights and penetration denominators.

source: HUD–USPS TRACT→ZIP crosswalk (residential ratio)
notes: Lifts tract-level ACS to ZIP grain; res_ratio weights. Vintage aligned to 2020 tracts and ACS 2023.

source: Zillow Home Value Index (ZHVI)
notes: Independent value cross-check (r = 0.89 vs ACS) — confirms the value rankings aren't a crosswalk artefact.

source: Median year built (ACS B25035) + population (B01003)
notes: Housing-vintage and density controls for the regression robustness ladder (Appendix B).

source: National Risk Index — wildfire EAL & social vulnerability
notes: DEFERRED — endpoint bot-blocked (403). Not used as a covariate; county fixed effects substitute.

source: Fire Hazard Severity Zones (FHSZ)
notes: Optional independent hazard layer (Path B). Not built in the core pipeline; FAIR risk score is Path A.

source: Annual acres/fires & structures destroyed
notes: Context charts on page 1. 2016 = six major fires only; 2025 preliminary; verify latest year at fire.ca.gov.

source: Top-20 largest / deadliest / most-destructive fires
notes: Bubble and bar charts on page 1. Santiago Canyon (1889) predates reliable records.

source: Live incident feed (current-year fires)
notes: 'Season right now' tables. Tracked incidents only; counts not comparable across years (acreage is). Refresh with fetch_fire_feed.py.

notes: Pre-2025 losses in 2025 dollars; 2025 fires in current dollars. Maui (HI) filtered out of the California chart.

notes: County-level retreat aggregates behind the county map and county non-renewal rates; the county companion to the ZIP file.

source: Residential dwelling units insured — FAIR Plan vs voluntary market
notes: Independent check on the FAIR share of the residential stock; context for the reliance and premium-vs-voluntary comparison (page 4).

source: P&C market-share summary / Top-25 groups / preface
notes: Market-concentration context and the methodology/definitions companion to the market-share workbooks used for the assessment base.

source: CA earthquake residential exposure & premium study
notes: CA insurance-market-size benchmark only — NOT wildfire and NOT in the panel. Included for corpus completeness.

notes: County aggregates behind the county choropleth; a consistency cross-check on the ZIP totals.

source: CDI Sustainable Insurance Strategy & wildfire-regulation corpus
notes: Regulatory context: SIS slides/fact sheet; distressed-county & undermarketed-ZIP commitment lists; catastrophe-modeling draft regulation; Safer-from-Wildfire FAQ. Background — not panel inputs.

source: CDI FAIR Plan oversight & market-conduct documents
